calendar.matters:
APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2017 Affirmed

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Continued To Jan-25-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Fitch (415-678-6915) appears as plaintiff/opposing party, In Propria Persona

Deputy City Attorney, Joshua S. White (415-554-4661) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the court reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff John Fitch fails to state a cause of action for breach of contract and his response/opposition fails to show that there is a reasonable possibility that he can plead a cause of action for breach of contract or any other claim. Plaintiff's complaint seeks injunctive relief that is now moot and in such circumstances a court in equity will not intercede. (Complaint 3:10-15; Lanahan v. City of Los Angeles (1939) 14 Cal.2d 128, 132.) Nor does plaintiff sufficiently allege offer, acceptance, consideration, or definite contractual terms. Plaintiff's complaint also appears to be based on the misapprehension that defendant should have listed plaintiff's name on the ballot even though plaintiff was running as a "write-in" candidate (Complaint 2:10-11.) By definition, plaintiff's name would not be listed on the ballot as voters need to "write-in" a write in a candidate's name. (Elections Code 302.) Plaintiff's case citations (Fair v. Hernandez (1981) 116 Cal.App.3d 868 and Wilburn v. Wixson (1974) 37 Cal.App.3d 730) do not support his asserted claim. In Fair the issue was whether certain ballots of a write-in candidate ought to have been counted. Wilburn was also an inapposite election contest case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Continued To Oct-26-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Special Appearance Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff John Fitch telephone number 415-678-6915 in Propria Persona.
Joshua White of Office of the City Attorney 415-554-4661 for Defendant Department of Elections.

DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS' Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. The Plaintiff will provide service to either the Mayor or the Board of Supervisors.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
